title: The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay
author: Michael Chabon

setting.note: The golden-age comic-book New York of two young cousins who invent a superhero to fight a war America has not yet joined. The map runs from a publisher's office high in the Empire State Building out to the tenement streets of Brooklyn, the bohemian rooms of Greenwich Village and the fairgrounds of Flushing Meadows, with Josef's Prague and a wartime Antarctic base held as far frames.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Michael Chabon, first published 2000. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay, Michael Chabon (2000). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The inked red of a golden-age comic-book cover, the colour of the Escapist's suit and of the pulp printed cheap and sold at a dime. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
